Container.Name: step-ca
Container.Overview:
Smallstep Step-CA — a self-hosted online Certificate Authority. This template is published alongside step-ui-ng (https://github.com/nofuturekid/step-ui-ng) as the CA half of the stack: run step-ca here, then point step-ui-ng at it to manage certificates, provisioners and ACME from a web UI.
IMPORTANT — the DOCKER_STEPCA_INIT_* variables are evaluated ONLY on the very first start, while the appdata path (/home/step) is still EMPTY. On an already-initialized CA they are ignored; config/ca.json is authoritative from then on.
First start (empty appdata): 1. Set "Init: DNS Names" to include every name/IP clients will use to reach the CA (your Unraid host IP and/or hostname) — these become the TLS SANs. Keep localhost,127.0.0.1 so the built-in healthcheck can validate locally. 2. Start the container, then open `docker logs step-ca`: it prints the admin username and admin password (the Remote-Management / JWK super-admin). Save these once — they are not shown again. 3. Get the root fingerprint to configure clients: docker exec step-ca step certificate fingerprint certs/root_ca.crt 4. In step-ui-ng enter the CA URL (https://HOST:9000) and that fingerprint.
Networking: this template defaults to bridge with published port 9000, so clients reach the CA at https://UNRAID-HOST:9000 — make sure that host IP/hostname is in the DNS Names. For a stable address, a custom br0 network with a fixed IP is recommended for production CAs.

Config.Name: CA Port
Config.Description: Port the CA's HTTPS API listens on. Clients (incl. step-ui-ng) connect to https://HOST:9000.

Config.Name: Appdata (CA state)
Config.Description: Persistent storage (bind mount): root/intermediate keys, certs, config/ca.json, secrets/password and the CA database. BACK THIS UP and restrict access. Deleting this directory means a full reinit — a NEW root CA, invalidating every certificate issued so far.

Config.Name: Init: CA Name
Config.Description: FIRST START ONLY (ignored once initialized). The CA name / certificate issuer.

Config.Name: Init: DNS Names
Config.Description: FIRST START ONLY. Comma-separated names/IPs the CA is reachable on; these become the CA's TLS SANs. ADD YOUR UNRAID HOST IP AND/OR HOSTNAME here (e.g. localhost,127.0.0.1,192.168.1.10,ca.example.lan) — clients can only verify TLS for names listed here. Keep localhost,127.0.0.1 so the built-in healthcheck works.

Config.Name: Init: Remote Management
Config.Description: FIRST START ONLY. true = enable the Admin API and a JWK super-admin (you get a password, not a cert). Required to manage provisioners at runtime (incl. from step-ui-ng).

Config.Name: Init: ACME Provisioner
Config.Description: FIRST START ONLY. true = create an initial ACME provisioner.

Config.Name: Init: SSH CA
Config.Description: FIRST START ONLY. true = also provision an SSH certificate authority (host/user CA).

Config.Name: Init: Provisioner Name
Config.Description: FIRST START ONLY. Name of the initial JWK admin provisioner.

Config.Name: Init: Password (optional)
Config.Description: FIRST START ONLY. Leave EMPTY (recommended): a strong password is generated and printed to docker logs. Only set this to provide the CA key password yourself (less secure than letting it be generated).
